summary: Analyzes retry storms under capped exponential backoff for optimistic-concurrency clients and shows via simulation that adding randomness to backoff reduces contention, total client work, and completion time. Defines the Full Jitter strategy as sleep = random_between(0, min(cap, base * 2^attempt)), i.e. the delay is drawn uniformly from [0, capped exponential], and compares it against Equal Jitter and Decorrelated Jitter. This post is the origin of the 'full jitter' terminology and formula used by retry libraries.
title: Exponential Backoff And Jitter
